hypothesis: Under a true null effect, checking an unadjusted paired significance test every 25 tasks until 400 tasks will exceed the nominal 5% false-positive rate, while fixed-horizon and Bonferroni-controlled analyses will remain at or below 5%.
claimBoundary: The study supports operating-characteristic claims for the specified paired Bernoulli simulation. It does not estimate any named model's quality, prove that Bonferroni is optimal, or cover dependence across benchmark tasks, judge-model errors, benchmark contamination, or adaptive task selection.

baselines: Fixed-horizon paired test performed once at the maximum task count
Naive repeated paired tests at every scheduled review using alpha 0.05
controls: Zero-effect null condition for false-positive calibration
Bonferroni alpha divided by the number of scheduled looks
